title: An interview with Jerry Hawthorne - From Heroscape to Wandering Galaxy
body:
This interview with Jerry Hawthorne (@Jerry.Hawthorne ) delves deep into his career as a game designer, tracing his roots back to early inspirations like HeroQuest and his progression into modern storytelling board games. Jerry discusses how HeroQuest sparked his interest in RPG-light, narrative-driven games and how that journey evolved through titles like Mice and Mystics, Stuffed Fables, and Aftermath. His approach to game design emphasizes storytelling, emotional depth, and accessibility, with a recurring theme of blending gameplay with narrative, as seen in his hallmark games that often cater to family audiences while still engaging adult players.

One of the key points of the interview is the evolution of Jerry’s design style, from early experimentation with hybrid board games and RPGs to the more sophisticated story-driven experiences that combine various elements of gameplay, including character progression, zone-based movement, and app integration. His shift toward integrating digital apps, seen in games like Familiar Tales and Wandering Galaxy, marks a significant step in his design philosophy, allowing for more dynamic and branching narratives while reducing the complexity of traditional game setups.

The conversation also touches on how Jerry draws inspiration from personal experiences and real-life emotions, such as grief and family dynamics, which subtly shape the themes of his games. Mice and Mystics, for example, was born out of a desire to help his daughter with reading, while Comanauts reflects a period in his life when he was dealing with his mother’s illness. These personal elements enrich his games with depth, making them more than just simple adventures.

The upcoming game Wandering Galaxy, set to release through a crowdfunding campaign, takes his storytelling to a new level by introducing more open-world, sandbox elements and cooperative gameplay. Players will be able to explore a feudalistic, spacefaring galaxy, engaging in combat, trading, bounty hunting, and colony management, all while navigating through a dynamic and narrative-rich world supported by app technology.

Jerry’s consistent innovation and evolution in game design, along with his focus on storytelling and emotional engagement, continue to make him a prominent figure in the modern board game industry.
